name: np-codeocean

metadata.name: np_codeocean
metadata.summary: Tools for uploading and interacting with Mindscope Neuropixels experiments on Code Ocean

documentation:
# np_codeocean
Tools for uploading and interacting with Mindscope Neuropixels experiments on Code Ocean

Requires running as admin on Windows in order to create remote-to-remote symlinks
on the Isilon.

- `upload` CLI tool is provided, which uses the
  [`np_session`](https://github.com/AllenInstitute/np_session) interface to find
  and upload
  raw data for one ecephys session:

    ```
    pip install np_codeocean
    upload <session-id>
    ```
 
    where session-id is a valid input to `np_session.Session()`: 
    - a lims ID (`1333741475`) 
    - a workgroups foldername (`DRPilot_366122_20230101`) 
    - a path to a session folder (    `\\allen\programs\mindscope\workgroups\np-exp\1333741475_719667_20240227`)
    
- a folder of symlinks pointing to the raw data is created, with a new structure suitable for the KS2.5 sorting pipeline on Code Ocean
- the symlink folder, plus metadata, are entered into a csv file, which is
  submitted to [`http://aind-data-transfer-service`](http://aind-data-transfer-service), which in turn runs the
  [`aind-data-transfer`](https://github.com/AllenNeuralDynamics/aind-data-transfer)
  tool on the HPC, which follows the symlinks to the original data,
  median-subtracts/scales/compresses ephys data, then uploads with the AWS CLI tool
- all compression/zipping acts on copies in temporary folders: the original raw data is not altered in anyway

functions.name: np_codeocean.metadata.utils.load_hdf5
documentation: Load hdf5 file from path.

functions.name: np_codeocean.metadata.utils.extract_hdf5_value
documentation:
Extract value from hdf5 file using a path. Path is a list of property
names that are used to traverse the hdf5 file. A path of length greater
than 1 is expected to point to a nested property.

functions.name: np_codeocean.metadata.utils.find_replace_or_append
documentation:
Find an item in a list of items that matches the filters and replace it.
If no item is found, append.

functions.name: np_codeocean.metadata.utils.save_aind_model
documentation:
Save aind models to a specified output path.

Notes
-----
- Gets around awkward `write_standard_file` method. to write to a determined
output path.

modules.name: np_codeocean.metadata.common
documentation: Models and controlled language for aind-data-schema devices names.

classes.name: np_codeocean.metadata.common.BehaviorBoxId
documentation: There is a divergence between the task rig id and the mpe rig id.

functions.name: np_codeocean.metadata.rigs.copy_rig
documentation:
Copies a rig from storage to `output_path`.

```pycon
>>> storage_directory = Path("examples") / "rig-directory"
>>> copy_rig("NP3", Path("rig.json"), datetime.datetime(2024, 4, 1), storage_directory)
PosixPath('rig.json')
```

# Notes

- If `storage_directory` is not provided, will attempt to get default from

> np-config.

functions.name: np_codeocean.metadata.rigs.is_behavior_box
documentation:
```pycon
>>> is_behavior_box("NP3")
False
>>> is_behavior_box("B2")
True
```

functions.name: np_codeocean.metadata.rigs.is_on_supported_cluster
documentation:
```pycon
>>> is_on_supported_cluster("NP3")
False
>>> is_on_supported_cluster("B2")
True
```

functions.name: np_codeocean.metadata.rigs.is_retrofitted_rig
documentation:
Retrofitted rigs are behavior box rigs that have a speaker attached and
have a different solenoid.

functions.name: np_codeocean.metadata.model_templates.behavior_box.init
documentation:
Initializes a rig model for an nsb behavior box.

```pycon
>>> rig_model = init("B6", datetime.date.today())
```

# Notes

- rig_id is expected to be in the format:
  : \<ROOM NAME>\_\<RIG NAME>\_\<MODIFICATION DATE>
- The DR task does not set the brightness and contrast of the monitor.

> These are hardcoded and assumed to be static.

functions.name: np_codeocean.metadata.model_templates.neuropixels_rig.init
documentation:
Initializes a rig model for the dynamic routing project.

```pycon
>>> rig_model = init("NP3")
```

# Notes

- rig_id is expected to be in the format:
  : \<ROOM NAME>\_\<RIG NAME>\_\<MODIFICATION DATE>
- The DR task does not set the brightness and contrast of the monitor.

> These are hardcoded and assumed to be static.

functions.name: np_codeocean.utils.get_project_config
documentation: Config for this project

functions.name: np_codeocean.utils.is_surface_channel_recording
documentation:
```pycon
>>> import np_session
>>> session = np_session.Session("//allen/programs/mindscope/workgroups/dynamicrouting/PilotEphys/Task 2 pilot/DRpilot_690706_20231129_surface_channels")
>>> is_surface_channel_recording(session.npexp_path.as_posix())
True
```

functions.name: np_codeocean.utils.cleanup_ephys_symlinks
documentation:
After creating symlinks to the ephys data, run this to make any necessary 
modifications prior to upload.

Provided dir path should be a directory containing all ephys data in
subfolders (e.g. directory containing "Record Node 10x" folders)

Only deletes symlinks or writes new files in place of symlinks - does not
modify original data.

Rules:
- if any continuous.dat files are unreadable: remove them and their containing folders
- if any probes were recorded on multiple record nodes: just keep the first
- if continuous.dat files are missing (ie. excluded because probes weren't
  inserted, or we removed symlinks in previous steps): update metadata files

functions.name: np_codeocean.utils.remove_folder_of_symlinks
documentation:
Recursive deletion of all files in dir tree, with a check that each is a
symlink.

functions.name: np_codeocean.utils.is_csv_in_hpc_upload_queue
documentation:
Check if an upload job has been submitted to the hpc upload queue.

- currently assumes one job per csv
- does not check status (job may be FINISHED rather than RUNNING)

```pycon
>>> is_csv_in_hpc_upload_queue("//allen/programs/mindscope/workgroups/np-exp/codeocean/DRpilot_664851_20231114/upload.csv")
False
```

functions.name: np_codeocean.utils.is_session_in_hpc_queue
documentation:
```pycon
>>> is_session_in_hpc_queue(366122, datetime.datetime(2023, 11, 14, 0, 0, 0))
False
>>> is_session_in_hpc_queue(702136, datetime.datetime(2024, 3, 4, 13, 21, 35))
True
```

functions.name: np_codeocean.utils.put_jobs_for_hpc_upload
documentation:
Submit one or more jobs to the aind-data-transfer-service, for
upload to S3 on the hpc.

- accepts one or more aind_data_schema BasicUploadJobConfigs models
- assembles a SubmitJobRequest model
- excludes jobs for sessions that are already in the upload queue
- accepts additional parameters for SubmitHpcJobRequest as kwargs
- submits json via http request
- optionally saves the json file as a record

functions.name: np_codeocean.utils.put_csv_for_hpc_upload
documentation:
Submit a single job upload csv to the aind-data-transfer-service, for
upload to S3 on the hpc.

- gets validated version of csv
- checks session is not already being uploaded
- submits csv via http request

functions.name: np_codeocean.utils.convert_symlinks_to_posix
documentation:
Convert all symlinks in `root_dir` (recursively) to POSIX paths. This is a
necessary last step before submitting uploads to run on the HPC.

variables.name: np_codeocean.utils.DEFAULT_EPHYS_SLURM_SETTINGS
documentation: Increased timelimit and cpus for running ephys compression on the hpc

modules.name: np_codeocean.metadata.storage
documentation:
Stores a history of object changes in local storage. Simple updater and
getter for iterations of a file.

functions.name: np_codeocean.metadata.storage.get_item
documentation: Gets latest item before or equal to timestamp.

functions.name: np_codeocean.metadata.storage.update_item
documentation:
Notes
-----
- Also used to initialize a new base rig in local storage.
- If a rig with the same name doest not exist, a new rig will be created.

functions.name: np_codeocean.metadata.update._run_neuropixels_rig_etl
documentation: Utility for running a neuropixels rig ETL and continuing on error.

functions.name: np_codeocean.metadata.update.update_rig_modification_date
documentation:
Convenience function that updates the modification date of an

: `aind-data-schema` `rig.json` and saves it over its current path.

```pycon
>>> update_rig_modification_date(
...     pathlib.Path(".", "examples", "rig.json"),
...     datetime.date(2024, 4, 1),
... )
PosixPath('examples/rig.json')
```

functions.name: np_codeocean.metadata.update.update_rig_room_number
documentation:
Convenience function that updates the room number of an

: `aind-data-schema` `rig.json` and saves it over its current path.

```pycon
>>> update_rig_room_number(
...     pathlib.Path(".", "examples", "rig.json"),
...     "324",
... )
PosixPath('examples/rig.json')
```

functions.name: np_codeocean.metadata.update.update_rig
documentation:
Updates a rig model file with the metadata from the given sources.

```pycon
>>> update_rig(
...  pathlib.Path(".", "examples", "rig.json"),
...  open_ephys_settings_sources=[
...     pathlib.Path(".", "examples", "settings.xml"),
...  ],
... )
PosixPath('rig.json')
```

# Notes

- \_source, if present will update various values in the rig model.

modules.name: np_codeocean.scripts.upload_split_recordings_example
documentation:
Linked to a .exe file in the virtual env, which can be run as admin to get around
sylink-creation permissions issues.

- just edit this file, then run the `upload_sessions.exe` as admin (~/.venv/scripts/upload_sessions.exe)

modules.name: np_codeocean.metadata.core
documentation:
Convenience functions for:
- Adding neuropixels rig to dynamic routing session directory.
- Updating neuropixels rig from dynamic routing session directory.

functions.name: np_codeocean.metadata.core.scrape_session_model_path
documentation:
Scrapes aind-metadata session json from dynamic routing session
directory.

functions.name: np_codeocean.metadata.core.update_session_from_rig
documentation:
Convenience function that updates the `rig_id` of a session model at
 `session_source`. Uses the `rig_id` of `rig_source`.

Notes
-----
- Overwrites the session model at `output_path`.

functions.name: np_codeocean.metadata.core.add_np_rig_to_session_dir
documentation:
Direct support for the dynamic routing task. Adds an `aind-data-schema`
 `rig.json` to a dynamic routing session directory. The `aind-data-schema`
 `session.json` in `session_dir` will be updated with the `rig_id` of the
  added `rig.json`.

Notes
-----
- An aind metadata session json must exist and be ending with filename
session.json (pattern: `*session.json`) in `session_dir`.
- If `rig_model_dir` is not provided, will attempt to get default from
 np-config. You will need to be onprem for `np-config` to work.

functions.name: np_codeocean.metadata.core.update_neuropixels_rig_from_dynamic_routing_session_dir
documentation:
Scrapes dynamic routing session directory for various rig
configuration/settings and updates `rig_source`.

Notes
-----
- Will likely be depreciated in the future.

functions.name: np_codeocean.metadata.core.extract_rig_name
documentation:
Extracts rig_name from task_source.

```pycon
>>> extract_rig_name(
...     pathlib.Path("examples") / "neuropixels-rig-task.hdf5"
... )
'NP2'
>>> extract_rig_name(
...     pathlib.Path("examples") / "behavior-box-task-0.hdf5"
... )
'D6'
>>> extract_rig_name(
...     pathlib.Path("examples") / "behavior-box-task-1.hdf5"
... )
'B2'
```

# Notes

- If extracted `computerName` is not found or is not bytes, will use

> `rigName`.

functions.name: np_codeocean.metadata.core.extract_session_datetime
documentation:
```pycon
>>> extract_session_datetime(
...     pathlib.Path("examples") / "behavior-box-task-0.hdf5"
... )
datetime.datetime(2024, 5, 1, 0, 0)
>>> extract_session_datetime(
...     pathlib.Path("examples") / "behavior-box-task-1.hdf5"
... )
datetime.datetime(2023, 9, 8, 0, 0)
```

functions.name: np_codeocean.metadata.core.copy_task_rig
documentation:
Extracts rig_name from task_source and copies the associated `rig.json`

: to output_path.

```pycon
>>> storage_directory = pathlib.Path("examples") / "rig-directory"
>>> task_source = pathlib.Path("examples") / "neuropixels-rig-task.hdf5"
>>> copy_task_rig(
...     task_source,
...     pathlib.Path("rig.json"),
...     storage_directory,
... )
PosixPath('rig.json')
```

```pycon
>>> task_source = pathlib.Path("examples") / "behavior-box-task-0.hdf5"
>>> copy_task_rig(
...     task_source,
...     pathlib.Path("rig.json"),
...     storage_directory,
... )
PosixPath('rig.json')
```

# Notes

- If `storage_directory` is not provided, will attempt to get default from

> np-config.

functions.name: np_codeocean.scripts.upload_dynamic_routing_ecephys.add_metadata
documentation: Adds rig and sessions metadata to a session directory.

functions.name: np_codeocean.scripts.upload_dynamic_routing_ecephys.write_metadata_and_upload
documentation:
Writes and updates aind-data-schema to the session directory
 associated with the `session`. The aind-data-schema session model is
 updated to reflect the `rig_id` of the rig model added to the session
 directory.

Only handles ecephys platform uploads (ie sessions with a folder of data; not 
behavior box sessions, which have a single hdf5 file only)

functions.name: np_codeocean.scripts.upload_dynamic_routing_behavior.add_metadata
documentation: Adds `aind-data-schema` rig and session metadata to a session directory.

functions.name: np_codeocean.scripts.upload_dynamic_routing_behavior.upload
documentation:
Notes
-----
- task_source Path is expected to have the following naming convention:
    //allen/programs/mindscope/workgroups/dynamicrouting/DynamicRoutingTask/Data/<SUBJECT_ID>/<SESSION_ID>.hdf5

functions.name: np_codeocean.np_session_utils.create_aind_metadata_symlinks
documentation:
Create symlinks in `dest` pointing to aind metadata json files from the root directory
on np-exp. Returns True if any metadata files are found in np-exp and the `aind_metadata`
folder is created.

functions.name: np_codeocean.np_session_utils.create_ephys_symlinks
documentation:
Create symlinks in `dest` pointing to raw ephys data files on np-exp, with only one
`recording` per `Record Node` folder (the largest, if multiple found).

Relative paths are preserved, so `dest` will essentially be a merge of
_probeABC / _probeDEF folders.

Top-level items other than `Record Node *` folders are excluded.

functions.name: np_codeocean.np_session_utils.create_behavior_symlinks
documentation:
Create symlinks in `dest` pointing to files in top-level of session
folder on np-exp, plus all files in `exp` subfolder, if present.

functions.name: np_codeocean.np_session_utils.create_behavior_videos_symlinks
documentation:
Create symlinks in `dest` pointing to MVR video files and info jsons in top-level of session
folder on np-exp.

functions.name: np_codeocean.np_session_utils.get_surface_channel_start_time
documentation:
```pycon
>>> session = np_session.Session("//allen/programs/mindscope/workgroups/dynamicrouting/PilotEphys/Task 2 pilot/DRpilot_690706_20231129_surface_channels")
>>> get_surface_channel_start_time(session)
datetime.datetime(2023, 11, 29, 14, 56, 25, 219000)
```

functions.name: np_codeocean.np_session_utils.get_upload_csv_for_session
documentation:
```pycon
>>> path = "//allen/programs/mindscope/workgroups/dynamicrouting/PilotEphys/Task 2 pilot/DRpilot_690706_20231129_surface_channels"
>>> utils.is_surface_channel_recording(path)
True
>>> upload = create_codeocean_upload(path)
>>> ephys_upload_csv = get_upload_csv_for_session(upload)
>>> ephys_upload_csv['modality0.source']
'//allen/programs/mindscope/workgroups/np-exp/codeocean/DRpilot_690706_20231129_surface_channels/ephys'
>>> ephys_upload_csv.keys()
dict_keys(['project_name', 'platform', 'subject-id', 'force_cloud_sync', 'modality0', 'modality0.source', 'acq-datetime'])
```

functions.name: np_codeocean.np_session_utils.get_np_session
documentation:
Accommodates surface channel folders, and updates the returned instance's
npexp_path accordingly

functions.name: np_codeocean.np_session_utils.create_codeocean_upload
documentation:
Create directories of symlinks to np-exp files with correct structure
for upload to CodeOcean.

- only one `recording` per `Record Node` folder (largest if multiple found)
- job file for feeding into `aind-data-transfer`

```pycon
>>> upload = create_codeocean_upload("//allen/programs/mindscope/workgroups/dynamicrouting/PilotEphys/Task 2 pilot/DRpilot_690706_20231129_surface_channels")
>>> upload.behavior is None
True
>>> upload.ephys.exists()
True
```

classes.name: np_codeocean.np_session_utils.CodeOceanUpload

class_variables.name: np_codeocean.np_session_utils.CodeOceanUpload.session
documentation: Session object that the paths belong to.

class_variables.name: np_codeocean.np_session_utils.CodeOceanUpload.platform
documentation:
The 'platform' in the Neural Dynamics data schema language (effectively the rig
type, which determines the processing pipeline the data follows).
 
Our rules are:
- if it ran in a behavior box: `behavior`
- anything else: `ecephys`

This means there will be behavior-only sessions that ran on NP-rigs
without ephys data (habs, opto experiments etc.), that will be uploaded as
`ecephys` platform data.

class_variables.name: np_codeocean.np_session_utils.CodeOceanUpload.behavior
documentation:
Directory of symlinks to files in top-level of session folder on np-exp,
plus all files in `exp` and `qc` subfolders, if present. Excludes behavior video files
and video info jsons.

class_variables.name: np_codeocean.np_session_utils.CodeOceanUpload.behavior_videos
documentation:
Directory of symlinks to behavior video files and video info jsons in
top-level of session folder on np-exp.

class_variables.name: np_codeocean.np_session_utils.CodeOceanUpload.ephys
documentation:
Directory of symlinks to raw ephys data files on np-exp, with only one
`recording` per `Record Node` folder.

class_variables.name: np_codeocean.np_session_utils.CodeOceanUpload.aind_metadata
documentation:
Directory of symlinks to aind metadata json files in top-level of session folder 
on np-exp.

class_variables.name: np_codeocean.np_session_utils.CodeOceanUpload.job
documentation: File containing job parameters for `aind-data-transfer`

class_variables.name: np_codeocean.np_session_utils.CodeOceanUpload.force_cloud_sync
documentation: If True, re-upload and re-make raw asset even if data exists on S3.

Objects required for uploading a Mindscope Neuropixels session to CodeOcean.
Paths are symlinks to files on np-exp.
